34.reason: [When the stack is empty, pop() returns 0, which is a valid integer. The calling code cannot distinguish between an actual popped value of 0 and an error condition. This leads to incorrect output when popping from an empty stack because the main function will print "0 " instead of handling it as an error. The reference program uses EOF to signal an error, but this implementation returns 0, causing misinterpretation in main().]

61.reason: [Because pop() returns 0 on error instead of a distinguishable error code (like EOF), this line will print "0 " even when the stack is empty, instead of just printing "error ". This causes incorrect output for pop operations on an empty stack.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.5 , reason:  [Because pop() returns 0 on error instead of a distinguishable error code (like EOF), this line will print "0 " even when the stack is empty, instead of just printing "error ". This causes incorrect output for pop operations on an empty stack.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.5 , reason:  [When pop() returns 0 due to an empty stack, this prints "0 " instead of handling the error properly. The problem requires printing "error " for invalid pop, but the current logic prints the return value regardless, leading to incorrect output.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.5 , reason:  [When pop() returns 0 due to an empty stack, this prints "0 ", but it should only print "error ". The error message is printed inside pop(), but then the return value (0) is also printed, leading to duplicate or incorrect output like "error 0 " or just "0 " instead of "error ".]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.5 , reason:  [When pop() returns 0 due to an empty stack, this prints "0 " instead of just "error ". The error message is already printed inside pop(), but then the return value (0) is also printed, leading to duplicate or incorrect output like "error 0 " or just "0 " if error printing is missed in logic flow. Also, the problem requires only "error " to be printed on underflow, not followed by a number.]
